violations.description: General employee practices. — --Food employee observed drinking/using open beverage container outside of designated area. Employee drink in the kitchen on the counter did not have a lid.

Food supplies. — --Food item obtained from unapproved source. Brown shell eggs in the white fridge were dated for 4/27, and in containers that had a best by date of December 16, 2023. These are from an unapproved source
Food source requirements. — --Eggs did not meet Grade B or higher standards. Eggs from an unlicensed establishment did not have verification of grading requirments.

Thawing potentially hazardous food. — --Potentially hazardous food was not thawed in an approved manner. A bag of queso was being thawed in a sink of water. No water was running. This food must be thawed under running water. This food item was being thawed in the rinse sink of the three compartment sinks, and not in the sanitizer. The sanitizer sink was not air gapped, and the air gapped sink was not being used to thaw the queso.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. A container of deli ham in the white fridge was dated 5/9. This was not discarded within the 10 days required.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. Boxes of 16 ounce plastic cups were stored on the floor by the chest freezer in the back rooms.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not provided in sufficient number and/or not accessible to allow convenient use by employees. Items were being stored in the hand sink in the kitchen.--Handwashing lavatory was used for purpose other than handwashing. Dishes were being stored in the hand sink in the kitchen

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder or bar soap. The hand sink in the kitchen did not have soap available. The soap dispenser was on the shelf above and appeared broken.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. A box of ice cream cones was stored on the floor in the back stock room.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. Boxes of compostable fiber bowls and plastic cups were stored on the floor by the chest freezer.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not provided in sufficient number and/or not accessible to allow convenient use by employees. The hand wash sink in the kitchen was blocked by dishes.
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
